title: Extra Crispy Ethiopian New Potatoes Pilaf
description: Fatty, shaggy, and cozy, this Ethiopian-inspired pilaf features new potatoes, a rich deglaze-fond, and is paleo-friendly, all cooked to perfection in a vessel like a donabe.
instructions: Heat the donabe over medium heat.
Add 2 tbsp of the oil and swirl it to coat the inside of the vessel.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it starts to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced ginger, cumin, coriander, and paprika. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the new potatoes, salt, black pepper, and 2 cups of water to the donabe.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the donabe with a lid.
Simmer for 15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Increase the heat to medium-high and stir in the remaining 2 tbsp of oil.
Cook for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the potatoes are crispy and golden brown.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
To serve, crumble or tear injera or flatbread into small pieces and use to scoop up the pilaf, if desired.
